seo.title: Kentucky Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Kentucky — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license kentucky, dental assistant certification kentucky, RDA kentucky, DANB CDA kentucky, dental assistant radiography kentucky

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Kentucky.

steps.title: Meet Education/Experience Requirements
steps.description: You must have graduated from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or have equivalent education, training and experience.

steps.title: Pass the Kentucky Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Complete and pass the Kentucky Jurisprudence Examination.

steps.title: Submit Application
steps.description: Submit a complete application for registration to the Kentucky Board of Dentistry.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Kentucky does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Kentucky require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Kentucky does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Kentucky?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Kentucky.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Kentucky?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Kentucky?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Kentucky. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Kentucky does not license dental assistants. Employing dentists register DAs during their own renewal.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Kentucky
hero.dek: Kentucky Dental Assistant registration requires graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program (or equivalent experience), successful completion of the Kentucky Jurisprudence Examination, and completion of the application process.
